title: How Contract (an blueprint's How)
description: Framework §4. The file-based authoring surface for an blueprint's How: the Why cascade (top decisions -> principles -> patterns), the application and infrastructure contracts, a reference to the repository layout model (§4.3), and the interface contracts (industry standards, §4.4). Mirrors the SHACL How shapes: a top decision requires rationale; a pattern must realise a principle; an infrastructure contract must satisfy the application contract; interface contracts are derived from the domain model.

blueprint.description: The blueprint this How belongs to (e.g. rest-api).

top_decisions.description: §4.1 Foundational choices, each carrying rationale. The most-upstream cell: expensive reasoning done once, inherited everywhere.

decision.description: What is decided (e.g. 'AppHost at the solution root').

licenses.description: Principle ids this decision makes apply.

enforced_by.description: Verification ids that check conformance to this decision.

principles.description: §4.1 Rules a top decision licenses, stated checkably. Earn-their-place: a principle must be applied by a work unit or enforced by a verification.

statement.description: The checkable rule (a vague principle cannot back a verification).

licensed_by.description: Top-decision ids that license this principle.

enforced_by.description: Verification ids that prove it holds. Required for the trace to be true once any work unit applies it.

patterns.description: §4.1 Concrete shapes that realise principles; what a work unit emits.

shape.description: The concrete shape (e.g. 'operations that can fail return Result<T>').

realizes.description: Principle ids this pattern implements (required, mirrors PatternShape).

application_contract.description: §4.2 The invariant code-shaping decisions. Stable across instances of the blueprint. Each statement must be checkable.

layering.description: The layering rule, outermost to innermost (e.g. ['api','application','domain']).

persistence_model.description: e.g. EF Core over SQL, event-sourced.

statements.description: The contract as checkable statements. Each must be precise enough that a verification can confirm it (§4.2) — a convention that cannot be checked cannot be a contract.

statement.description: A checkable assertion (e.g. 'dependencies point inward; api may not be referenced by domain').

enforced_by.description: The verification that checks this statement.

infrastructure_contract.description: §4.2 Concrete runtime choices for an instance; frozen once chosen. Must satisfy the application contract.

satisfies.description: The application_contract id this satisfies (the conformsTo link).

frozen.description: Must be true: chosen per instance and frozen at Discovery.

resources.description: The concrete resources (compute, data, identity, secrets, messaging).

kind.description: e.g. compute / data-engine / identity / secrets / messaging.

choice.description: The concrete selection (e.g. Azure Container Apps, PostgreSQL).

satisfies_statement.description: The application-contract statement this choice satisfies (e.g. 'SQL domain model').

depends_on.description: Provisioning order: resources this one depends on.

layout_model.description: §4.3 Path/reference to the repository layout model file (validated by layout-model.schema.json).

interface_contracts.description: §4.4 Published interfaces, each described in an industry standard and generated from the domain model.

derived_from.description: Domain/event model elements this contract is generated from (never hand-written).
